title: screenshot
description: Takes a screenshot in PNG format.

anyOf.title: Screenshot (simple)
anyOf.$comment: The pattern gates the target's EXTENSION, not its characters or location: any path ending in .png/.PNG is accepted, plus an http(s) URL to a PNG (optionally with a query string, for signed URLs) and a `$VAR` reference. It deliberately does not restrict the character set — an earlier pattern allowed any Windows absolute path but limited non-Windows paths to [A-Za-z0-9_./\-], so ordinary macOS/Linux projects (a space, parentheses, '~', or non-ASCII anywhere in the path) failed validation, including for the absolute paths doc-detective synthesizes internally for autoScreenshot and recording checkpoints. That charset never bounded WHERE a screenshot could be written (traversal like ../../x.png always passed), so it only made non-Windows projects second-class. See ADR 01077.
anyOf.description: File path of the PNG file. Accepts absolute paths. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step. If an `http(s)` URL is supplied, the remote image is downloaded and used as a read-only reference for comparison; the new capture is written to a local run-specific folder instead of being uploaded back to the URL.

anyOf.$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] — NOT a sibling allOf inside the object — because the TS type generator collapses an object schema that carries a sibling allOf into an index-signature placeholder, dropping every field from the public types (same wrapping pattern as type_v3's kind-shaped guards).

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window to capture. Omit to capture the active surface — the most recently opened, focused, or explicitly targeted surface, whatever its kind (a background process can't be captured). Specifying a surface switches the active surface: it stays targeted for later surface-less steps. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }). App captures don't support `crop` yet.

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.
tab.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

app.description: Name of an app surface opened by `startSurface` (its `name`, or the default derived from the app identifier).

window.description: Which app window to act on. Omit to use the active window. Apps have windows, no tabs.
window.title: App window selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest (e.g. a dialog the app just opened).

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Assigned window name. The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Window title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

path.title: Screenshot (simple)
path.$comment: The pattern gates the target's EXTENSION, not its characters or location: any path ending in .png/.PNG is accepted, plus an http(s) URL to a PNG (optionally with a query string, for signed URLs) and a `$VAR` reference. It deliberately does not restrict the character set — an earlier pattern allowed any Windows absolute path but limited non-Windows paths to [A-Za-z0-9_./\-], so ordinary macOS/Linux projects (a space, parentheses, '~', or non-ASCII anywhere in the path) failed validation, including for the absolute paths doc-detective synthesizes internally for autoScreenshot and recording checkpoints. That charset never bounded WHERE a screenshot could be written (traversal like ../../x.png always passed), so it only made non-Windows projects second-class. See ADR 01077.
path.description: File path of the PNG file. Accepts absolute paths. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step. If an `http(s)` URL is supplied, the remote image is downloaded and used as a read-only reference for comparison; the new capture is written to a local run-specific folder instead of being uploaded back to the URL.

directory.description: Directory of the PNG file. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation in percentage of pixels between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot at `path`. If the difference between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If a screenshot doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing screenshot at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing screenshot at `path` if the difference between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot is greater than `maxVariation`.

anyOf.title: Crop by element (simple)
anyOf.description: Display text or selector of the element to screenshot.

anyOf.title: Crop by element (detailed)
anyOf.description: Crop the screenshot to a specific element.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to screenshot.

selector.description: Selector of the element to screenshot.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

anyOf.title: Padding (simple)
anyOf.description: Padding in pixels to add to the bounds of the element.

anyOf.title: Padding (detailed)
anyOf.description: Padding in pixels to add to the bounds of the element.

annotations.description: Visual annotations to draw onto the screenshot. Annotations are composited into the image and never touch the page, so they can't disturb the page under test or appear in a recording that's running at the same time. Each annotation is resolved against the capture after any `crop`. To keep annotations on screen across steps and in recordings, use an `annotate` step instead.

items.title: annotation
items.description: A visual annotation drawn onto a screenshot or recording. Each annotation names exactly one type (`outline`, `arrow`, `badge`, `callout`, `blur`, or `text`), and the type's value is the target it points at: an element (a selector/display-text string or a detailed find object) or a fixed `position` in the capture. `id`, `track`, `transition`, and `duration` describe behavior over time — they apply to recordings and are inert in still screenshots, so the same annotation means the same thing in both.
items.$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] — NOT a sibling anyOf/oneOf inside the object — because the TS type generator collapses an object schema that carries sibling composition keywords into an index-signature placeholder, dropping every field from the public types (same wrapping pattern as screenshot_v3's object and type_v3's kind-shaped guards). Both halves are written INLINE rather than $ref'd into components: the dereferencer inlines them into the root anyway AND keeps the components copy, so routing them through components doubled this schema's size in every consumer.

outline.description: Draw a box around the target.

anyOf.title: Target by element (simple)
anyOf.description: Display text or selector of the element to annotate.

anyOf.title: Target by element (detailed)
anyOf.description: Element to annotate. Mirrors the element-finding fields used elsewhere. On app surfaces only the natively-mappable fields are supported (`elementText`, `elementId`, `elementTestId`, `elementAria`); `selector`, `elementClass`, and `elementAttribute` have no native equivalent.
anyOf.$comment: allOf [shape, guard] for the same reason as the root schema: a sibling anyOf next to `properties` collapses the generated TS type to an index signature, which would erase every field here AND swallow the sibling target branches in the Target union (an index-signature type matches any object). crop_element in screenshot_v3 predates this and is still collapsed.

selector.description: Selector of the element to annotate. Browser surfaces only.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to annotate. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to annotate. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to annotate. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes. Browser surfaces only.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.
elementAttribute.$comment: Scalar branches precede the string branch: AJV runs with coerceTypes, so a leading string branch would coerce 5 into "5" and a number-valued attribute would silently change type before the runtime compared it. (crop_element in screenshot_v3 lists string first and predates this rule.)

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

anyOf.title: Target by position
anyOf.description: A fixed spot in the capture, for annotations that aren't anchored to an element.

anyOf.title: Named region
anyOf.description: A named spot, relative to the target element when the annotation has one, or to the capture when it doesn't.

anyOf.title: Annotation point
anyOf.$comment: Titled 'Annotation point' rather than 'Point' because the docs generator keys its reference pages by title — a bare 'Point' collides with swipe_v3's Point and silently overwrites that page. Not $ref'd from swipe_v3 either: its point is an integer with minimum 0 (a tap coordinate), while an annotation point is a fractional position in a capture.
anyOf.description: An absolute coordinate in the capture, in pixels from the top-left.

x.description: Horizontal position in pixels from the left edge of the capture.

y.description: Vertical position in pixels from the top edge of the capture.

arrow.description: Point an arrow at the target.

label.description: Text to display. Required by `badge`, `callout`, and `text`; ignored by the other types.

id.description: Handle for this annotation, so a later `annotate` step can update or clear it. Only meaningful for annotations added by an `annotate` step; ignored on screenshot annotations, which live only for the capture.

style.description: Visual overrides for this annotation. Anything unset falls back to the resolved `annotationDefaults` theme (test, then spec, then config), then to the built-in theme.
style.title: Annotation style
style.$comment: Deliberately free of `default` keywords. This schema is shared by annotation objects and by the annotationDefaults theme at the config, spec, and test levels; AJV's useDefaults would inject any default here into all of them, populating the spec/test levels and destroying the 'absent means defer to the level above' cascade. The built-in theme lives in the runtime instead.

color.description: Foreground color — strokes, arrowheads, and text (hex, rgb, or named color).

background.description: Background color for text-bearing annotations (hex, rgb, or named color). Use `transparent` for none.

fontFamily.description: Font family. Falls back through the list as in CSS.

radius.description: Corner radius in pixels, for boxes and text backgrounds.

padding.description: Padding in pixels inside text boxes, and between an outline and its element.

maxWidth.description: Maximum width in pixels for text before it wraps.

intensity.description: Blur strength. Higher values obscure more.

position.description: Where to place the annotation relative to its target. Accepts a named region, an absolute point, or an offset that nudges the default placement.

anyOf.title: Named region
anyOf.description: A named spot, relative to the target element when the annotation has one, or to the capture when it doesn't.

x.description: Horizontal position in pixels from the left edge of the capture.

y.description: Vertical position in pixels from the top edge of the capture.

anyOf.title: Annotation offset
anyOf.description: A nudge in pixels from the annotation's default placement.

offset.description: Pixels to shift the annotation by.

x.description: Pixels to shift right. Negative values shift left.

y.description: Pixels to shift down. Negative values shift up.

track.description: If `true`, the annotation follows its element as the page scrolls or reflows. Applies to recordings; inert in still screenshots, which capture a single moment.

transition.description: How the annotation enters and leaves. Applies to recordings; inert in still screenshots, which render the settled state.
transition.title: Annotation transition

enter.description: How the annotation appears. Use `none` for annotations that must never reveal what they cover — a `blur` that fades in shows the sensitive content underneath while it does.

durationMs.description: Length of the enter and exit animations, in milliseconds.

duration.description: Milliseconds to display the annotation before it clears itself. Omit to display it until an `annotate` step clears it. Applies to recordings; inert in still screenshots.

all.description: If `true`, annotates every element matching the target instead of only the first. Most useful with `blur`, where redacting only the first match can leave sensitive content visible.

allOf.title: Exactly one annotation type
allOf.$comment: oneOf over single-key `required` branches yields exactly-one semantics: two type keys satisfy two branches (oneOf fails), and zero satisfy none. An annotation naming two shapes is ambiguous about what to draw, so it's rejected at validation time rather than guessed at.

target.description: What the annotation points at: an element, or a fixed position in the capture.

anyOf.$comment: allOf [shape, guard] for the same reason as the root schema: a sibling anyOf next to `properties` collapses the generated TS type to an index signature, which would erase every field here AND swallow the sibling target branches in the Target union (an index-signature type matches any object). crop_element in screenshot_v3 predates this and is still collapsed.

selector.description: Selector of the element to annotate. Browser surfaces only.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to annotate. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to annotate. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to annotate. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes. Browser surfaces only.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.
elementAttribute.$comment: Scalar branches precede the string branch: AJV runs with coerceTypes, so a leading string branch would coerce 5 into "5" and a number-valued attribute would silently change type before the runtime compared it. (crop_element in screenshot_v3 lists string first and predates this rule.)

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

anyOf.$comment: Titled 'Annotation point' rather than 'Point' because the docs generator keys its reference pages by title — a bare 'Point' collides with swipe_v3's Point and silently overwrites that page. style.title: Annotation style
style.description: Visual properties. Every field is optional; unset fields fall back to the resolved theme, then the built-in theme.
style.$comment: Deliberately free of `default` keywords. This schema is shared by annotation objects and by the annotationDefaults theme at the config, spec, and test levels; AJV's useDefaults would inject any default here into all of them, populating the spec/test levels and destroying the 'absent means defer to the level above' cascade. The built-in theme lives in the runtime instead.

color.description: Foreground color — strokes, arrowheads, and text (hex, rgb, or named color).

background.description: Background color for text-bearing annotations (hex, rgb, or named color). Use `transparent` for none.

fontFamily.description: Font family. Falls back through the list as in CSS.

radius.description: Corner radius in pixels, for boxes and text backgrounds.

padding.description: Padding in pixels inside text boxes, and between an outline and its element.

maxWidth.description: Maximum width in pixels for text before it wraps.

intensity.description: Blur strength. Higher values obscure more.

transition.title: Annotation transition
transition.description: How an annotation enters and leaves during a recording.

enter.description: How the annotation appears. Use `none` for annotations that must never reveal what they cover — a `blur` that fades in shows the sensitive content underneath while it does.

durationMs.description: Length of the enter and exit animations, in milliseconds.

examples.label: Never stored — sent straight to the payment processor

sourceIntegration.description: Information about the source integration for this screenshot, enabling upload of changed files back to the source CMS. Set automatically during test resolution for files from integrations.
sourceIntegration.title: sourceIntegration

type.description: The type of integration. Currently supported: 'heretto'. Additional types may be added in the future.

integrationName.description: The name of the integration configuration in the config file. Used to look up authentication credentials.

fileId.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the file in the source CMS. If not provided, the file will be looked up by path.

filePath.description: The path of the file in the source CMS. Used for lookup if fileId is not available.

contentPath.description: The local path to the file that references this source. Used for resolving relative paths.

allOf.title: App captures don't support crop yet
allOf.$comment: crop relies on browser viewport APIs; on app surfaces it lands in a later phase. Rejected at validation time so authors don't hit a runtime FAIL.

anyOf.title: Capture screenshot (detailed)
Capture screenshot
anyOf.description: If `true`, captures a screenshot. If `false`, doesn't capture a screenshot.

path.title: Screenshot (simple)
path.$comment: The pattern gates the target's EXTENSION, not its characters or location: any path ending in .png/.PNG is accepted, plus an http(s) URL to a PNG (optionally with a query string, for signed URLs) and a `$VAR` reference. It deliberately does not restrict the character set — an earlier pattern allowed any Windows absolute path but limited non-Windows paths to [A-Za-z0-9_./\-], so ordinary macOS/Linux projects (a space, parentheses, '~', or non-ASCII anywhere in the path) failed validation, including for the absolute paths doc-detective synthesizes internally for autoScreenshot and recording checkpoints. That charset never bounded WHERE a screenshot could be written (traversal like ../../x.png always passed), so it only made non-Windows projects second-class. See ADR 01077.
path.description: File path of the PNG file. Accepts absolute paths. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step. If an `http(s)` URL is supplied, the remote image is downloaded and used as a read-only reference for comparison; the new capture is written to a local run-specific folder instead of being uploaded back to the URL.

object.$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] — NOT a sibling allOf inside the object — because the TS type generator collapses an object schema that carries a sibling allOf into an index-signature placeholder, dropping every field from the public types (same wrapping pattern as type_v3's kind-shaped guards).

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window to capture. Omit to capture the active surface — the most recently opened, focused, or explicitly targeted surface, whatever its kind (a background process can't be captured). Specifying a surface switches the active surface: it stays targeted for later surface-less steps. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }). App captures don't support `crop` yet.

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index
anyOf.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end; `-1` is the newest.

anyOf.title: By name
anyOf.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened (goTo `newTab`/`newWindow`). The integer branch is listed first because Ajv validates with coerceTypes — string-first would coerce integer indexes into name strings.

name.description: Name assigned when the window/tab was opened.

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

title.description: Page title to match. Substring, or /regex/.

url.description: Page URL to match. Substring, or /regex/.

tab.description: Which tab to act on. Omit to use the active tab. Without `window`, the selector searches every tab in creation order — including tabs the page opened itself.

directory.description: Directory of the PNG file. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation in percentage of pixels between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot at `path`. If the difference between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If a screenshot doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing screenshot at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing screenshot at `path` if the difference between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot is greater than `maxVariation`.

anyOf.description: Padding in pixels to add to the bounds of the element.

annotations.description: Visual annotations to draw onto the screenshot. Annotations are composited into the image and never touch the page, so they can't disturb the page under test or appear in a recording that's running at the same time. Each annotation is resolved against the capture after any `crop`. To keep annotations on screen across steps and in recordings, use an `annotate` step instead.

items.title: annotation
items.description: A visual annotation drawn onto a screenshot or recording. Each annotation names exactly one type (`outline`, `arrow`, `badge`, `callout`, `blur`, or `text`), and the type's value is the target it points at: an element (a selector/display-text string or a detailed find object) or a fixed `position` in the capture. `id`, `track`, `transition`, and `duration` describe behavior over time — they apply to recordings and are inert in still screenshots, so the same annotation means the same thing in both.
items.$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] — NOT a sibling anyOf/oneOf inside the object — because the TS type generator collapses an object schema that carries sibling composition keywords into an index-signature placeholder, dropping every field from the public types (same wrapping pattern as screenshot_v3's object and type_v3's kind-shaped guards). Both halves are written INLINE rather than $ref'd into components: the dereferencer inlines them into the root anyway AND keeps the components copy, so routing them through components doubled this schema's size in every consumer.

outline.description: Draw a box around the target.

object.title: Capture screenshot (detailed)

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window to capture. Omit to capture the active surface — the most recently opened, focused, or explicitly targeted surface, whatever its kind (a background process can't be captured). Specifying a surface switches the active surface: it stays targeted for later surface-less steps. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }). App captures don't support `crop` yet.

anyOf.title: Surface (by browser engine)
anyOf.description: Browser engine keyword. Targets that browser. Steps that can only ever act on a browser (not a background process) restrict the bare-string form to this enum, so a process name here is rejected at validation time instead of failing at runtime.

browser.description: Browser engine. Selects the browser surface with that engine (or the one named by `name`). A goTo step opens the browser if it isn't open yet — you can also open one explicitly with `startSurface`; other steps require it to already be open.

name.description: Name of the browser surface. Defaults to the engine name (the context's default browser registers under its engine). Assign distinct names to drive multiple browsers at once, including several of the same engine.

window.description: Which window to act on. Omit to use the active window.
window.title: Window/tab selector

anyOf.title: By index

anyOf.title: By name

index.description: Index in creation order. Negative counts from the end.

path.title: Screenshot (simple)
path.$comment: The pattern gates the target's EXTENSION, not its characters or location: any path ending in .png/.PNG is accepted, plus an http(s) URL to a PNG (optionally with a query string, for signed URLs) and a `$VAR` reference. It deliberately does not restrict the character set — an earlier pattern allowed any Windows absolute path but limited non-Windows paths to [A-Za-z0-9_./\-], so ordinary macOS/Linux projects (a space, parentheses, '~', or non-ASCII anywhere in the path) failed validation, including for the absolute paths doc-detective synthesizes internally for autoScreenshot and recording checkpoints. That charset never bounded WHERE a screenshot could be written (traversal like ../../x.png always passed), so it only made non-Windows projects second-class. See ADR 01077.
path.description: File path of the PNG file. Accepts absolute paths. If not specified, the file name is the ID of the step. If an `http(s)` URL is supplied, the remote image is downloaded and used as a read-only reference for comparison; the new capture is written to a local run-specific folder instead of being uploaded back to the URL.

directory.description: Directory of the PNG file. If the directory doesn't exist, creates the directory.

maxVariation.description: Allowed variation in percentage of pixels between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot at `path`. If the difference between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot is greater than `maxVariation`, the step fails. If a screenshot doesn't exist at `path`, this value is ignored.

overwrite.description:
If `true`, overwrites the existing screenshot at `path` if it exists.
If `aboveVariation`, overwrites the existing screenshot at `path` if the difference between the new screenshot and the existing screenshot is greater than `maxVariation`.

annotations.description: Visual annotations to draw onto the screenshot. Annotations are composited into the image and never touch the page, so they can't disturb the page under test or appear in a recording that's running at the same time. Each annotation is resolved against the capture after any `crop`. To keep annotations on screen across steps and in recordings, use an `annotate` step instead.

items.title: annotation
items.description: A visual annotation drawn onto a screenshot or recording. Each annotation names exactly one type (`outline`, `arrow`, `badge`, `callout`, `blur`, or `text`), and the type's value is the target it points at: an element (a selector/display-text string or a detailed find object) or a fixed `position` in the capture. `id`, `track`, `transition`, and `duration` describe behavior over time — they apply to recordings and are inert in still screenshots, so the same annotation means the same thing in both.
items.$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] — NOT a sibling anyOf/oneOf inside the object — because the TS type generator collapses an object schema that carries sibling composition keywords into an index-signature placeholder, dropping every field from the public types (same wrapping pattern as screenshot_v3's object and type_v3's kind-shaped guards). Both halves are written INLINE rather than $ref'd into components: the dereferencer inlines them into the root anyway AND keeps the components copy, so routing them through components doubled this schema's size in every consumer.

outline.description: Draw a box around the target.

radius.description: Corner radius in pixels, for boxes and text backgrounds.

padding.description: Padding in pixels inside text boxes, and between an outline and its element.

track.description: If `true`, the annotation follows its element as the page scrolls or reflows. Applies to recordings; inert in still screenshots, which capture a single moment.

transition.title: Annotation transition

enter.description: How the annotation appears. Use `none` for annotations that must never reveal what they cover — a `blur` that fades in shows the sensitive content underneath while it does.

durationMs.description: Length of the enter and exit animations, in milliseconds.

duration.description: Milliseconds to display the annotation before it clears itself. Omit to display it until an `annotate` step clears it. Applies to recordings; inert in still screenshots.

all.description: If `true`, annotates every element matching the target instead of only the first. Most useful with `blur`, where redacting only the first match can leave sensitive content visible.

allOf.title: Exactly one annotation type
allOf.$comment: oneOf over single-key `required` branches yields exactly-one semantics: two type keys satisfy two branches (oneOf fails), and zero satisfy none. An annotation naming two shapes is ambiguous about what to draw, so it's rejected at validation time rather than guessed at.

fileId.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the file in the source CMS. If not provided, the file will be looked up by path.

filePath.description: The path of the file in the source CMS. Used for lookup if fileId is not available.

contentPath.description: The local path to the file that references this source. Used for resolving relative paths.

crop_element.title: Crop by element (detailed)
crop_element.description: Crop the screenshot to a specific element.

elementText.description: Display text of the element to screenshot.

selector.description: Selector of the element to screenshot.

elementId.description: ID attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementTestId.description: data-testid attribute of the element to find. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

elementClass.description: Class or array of classes that the element must have. Each class supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax. Element must have all specified classes.

elementAttribute.description: Object of attribute key-value pairs that the element must have. Values can be strings (supporting /pattern/ regex), numbers, or booleans. Boolean true matches attribute presence, false matches absence.

elementAria.description: Computed accessible name of the element per ARIA specification. Supports exact match or regex pattern using /pattern/ syntax.

anyOf.title: Padding (simple)
anyOf.description: Padding in pixels to add to the bounds of the element.

anyOf.title: Padding (detailed)
anyOf.description: Padding in pixels to add to the bounds of the element.

padding.title: Padding (detailed)
padding.description: Padding in pixels to add to the bounds of the element.
